Benchmark (BM)
A fixed reference point of known elevation used as the starting point for levelling work.
Contour line
An imaginary line on a map joining points of equal elevation above a datum.
Levelling
The process of determining differences in elevation between points on the earth's surface.
Back sight (BS)
The first staff reading taken after setting up the level, on a point of known elevation.
Fore sight (FS)
The last staff reading taken before shifting the instrument, on a point whose elevation is to be found.
Theodolite
A precision instrument for measuring horizontal and vertical angles in traversing and triangulation.
Total station
An electronic instrument combining a theodolite with an EDM to measure angles and distances together.
Ranging
The process of establishing intermediate points on a straight line between two survey stations.
Plane table surveying
A graphical method of surveying in which field work and plotting are done simultaneously.
GPS
Global Positioning System — satellite-based positioning used for locating survey points.
